Editor.comment: `new Editor` returns your editor-instance containing a JSON Schema tree, registered widgets and plugins and the overall state of the data.

tags.comment:
import { Editor } from '@sagold/react-json-editor';

const editor = new Editor(options);
Each JSON Schema requires a valid JSON Schema type keyword
Returns an `editor` instance

properties.comment: Editor root node
properties.name: root

properties.comment: JSON Schema API
properties.name: schemaNode

properties.comment: JSON Schema API options
properties.name: schemaNodeConfig

properties.comment: list of active plugins
properties.name: plugins

properties.comment: input options for this editor instance
properties.name: options

methods.comment: Returns the current json data

tags.comment: A JSON Pointer string of the data to return. Use `undefined` or `""` to get all data or specify a path like `/header` or `/todo/4` to get the data from the requested location
tags.name: pointer

methods.name: getData

methods.comment: Get current data or a subset of the data specified by a JSON Pointer

tags.comment: to set
tags.name: data

tags.comment: new root node

methods.name: setData

methods.comment: Shortcut to return default data based on json-schema only

tags.comment: json-schema to get default data from. Defaults to current schema
tags.name: schema

tags.comment: optional data to merge with default data
tags.name: data

tags.comment: default data confirming to json-schema

methods.name: getTemplateData

tags.comment: Input JSON Schema of the current form

methods.name: getSchema

methods.comment: Replace the JSON Schema and recreate the JsonNode tree of the form

tags.comment: JSON Schema of the new form
tags.name: schema

tags.comment: The new root node for the given JSON Schema

methods.name: setSchema

methods.comment: Run validation for the current form and update the list of errors

tags.comment: List of validation errors. See [getErrors](#getErrors) for details

methods.name: validate

methods.comment:
Get current validation errors of node-tree.
An error contains the following properties
`{ type: "error", code: string, message: string, data }`.
The property `data` contains details to the error.
For further details check [json-schema-library#validate](https://github.com/sagold/json-schema-library#validate)

tags.comment: Current list of form validation errors of the current state.

methods.name: getErrors

methods.comment: Set new editor state (new tree of nodes)

tags.comment: new root node

methods.name: setState

methods.comment: Get current root-node

tags.comment: root node

methods.text: getNode(): JsonNode;
methods.name: getNode

methods.comment: Get current node at json-pointer location

tags.comment: node at requested location or json-error

methods.text: JsonNode | JsonError
methods.name: getNode
getNode

methods.comment: Get a plugin by pluginId
methods.name: findPlugin

methods.text: ReturnType<T> | undefined
methods.name: addPlugin

methods.comment: Runs all plugins per given change, allowing them to return a modified newState

tags.comment: The new root node

methods.name: runPlugins

methods.comment: Set or replace a value at given data location

tags.comment: json-pointer in data of target value
tags.name: pointer

tags.comment: value to set at location
tags.name: value

tags.comment: new root node or current one if nothing changed

methods.name: setValue

methods.comment: Add a value at a given location. This usually is used to add array-items

tags.comment: addValue **only supports adding properties**
A JSON Pointer string to the value to add
tags.name: pointer

tags.comment: The new root node

methods.name: addValue

methods.comment: Delete the value at the given JSON Pointer

tags.comment: A JSON Pointer string to the node which should be removed. Specify a data path like `/header` or `/todo/4` to remove the node from the specified location
tags.name: pointer

tags.comment: The new root node

methods.name: removeValue

methods.comment: Move the referenced **array-item** to another array position

tags.comment: A JSON Pointer string to the node
tags.name: pointer

tags.comment: The index to move the array item to
tags.name: to

tags.comment: The new root node

methods.name: moveItem

methods.comment: Add an item defined by a JSON Schema to the end of the target array

tags.comment: ArrayNode of item
tags.name: node

tags.comment: JSON Schema of the item to add
tags.name: itemSchema

tags.comment: The new root node

methods.name: appendItem

methods.comment: Get all available items that can be added next to the specified array

tags.comment: List of available JSON subschemas to insert into the array. Access the JSON Schema of each option by `SchemaNode.schema`. For more details see [json-schema-library#schemanode-methods](https://github.com/sagold/json-schema-library#schemanode-methods)

methods.name: getArrayAddOptions

methods.comment: destroys editor instance including all plugins, releasing used memory.

tags.comment: plugins will receive an onDestroy call once and are responsible to release memory on their own

methods.name: destroy

methods.comment: Use `editor.getWidget` to find a widget matching the requested `JsonNode`

tags.comment: is a JsonNode belonging to the root JsonNode of this editor, or the root itself. You can get the root by calling `editor.getNode()` or any nested root by specifying a JSON Pointer to the data `editor.getNode("#/header/title")`;
tags.name: node

tags.comment: can be passed to the lookup function, which will be available in a WidgetPlugin's use-function: `use(node, options) => boolean`. Usually, options are passed to getWidget as well as to the widget itself
tags.name: options

tags.comment: The **Widget component** to render the given node or undefined

methods.name: getWidget

Editor.name: Editor

properties.comment: The JSON Schema describing the form and its resulting data. JSON Schema `type` keywords are required for json-editor to work correctly.
properties.name: schema

properties.comment: The initial, partial data to be used to fill the form with values. The data has to match the JSON Schema or it will be ignored or invalidated in the we form
properties.name: data

properties.comment: if data should be initially validated
properties.name: validate

properties.comment: if all optional properties should be added when missing
properties.name: addOptionalProps

properties.comment: if json-schema default-values should be extended (required properties, min-length of items). Defaults to false
properties.name: extendDefaults

properties.comment: Remove data that does not match input schema. Defaults to false
properties.name: removeInvalidData

properties.comment: The list of available widgets to render the JSON Schema tree. Each node in the tree should have a matching widget to be rendered or the editor will either ignore or show an error using an Error Widget if added.
properties.name: widgets

properties.comment: Set to `true` to update form data on each keystroke instead of on blur. Only applied to supporting editors
properties.name: liveUpdate

properties.comment: Set to true to deactivate all forms
properties.name: disabled

setDefaultWidgets.comment: Register a list of widgets for all new editor instances per default
setDefaultWidgets.name: setDefaultWidgets

useEditor.comment:
Create an editor-instance containing a JSON Schema tree, registered widgets and plugins and the
overall state of the data.

tags.comment: when running React.StrictMode the returned editor instance will be undefined on the first call
Each JSON Schema requires a valid JSON Schema type keyword
Returns an instance of `Editor`. For details on working with `editor` see Usage below or more in depth details in API Reference on [Editor#instance](/docs/api-editor--docs#instance)

useEditor.name: useEditor

properties.comment: The JSON Schema describing the form and its resulting data. JSON Schema `type` keywords are required for json-editor to work correctly.
properties.name: schema

properties.comment: The initial, partial data to be used to fill the form with values. The data has to match the JSON Schema or it will be ignored or invalidated in the we form
properties.name: data

properties.comment: if data should be initially validated
properties.name: validate

properties.comment: if all optional properties should be added when missing
properties.name: addOptionalProps

properties.comment: if json-schema default-values should be extended (required properties, min-length of items). Defaults to false
properties.name: extendDefaults

properties.comment: Remove data that does not match input schema. Defaults to false
properties.name: removeInvalidData

properties.comment:
A function called after each udpate which that accepts three arguments:

- `data` the latest data of the web form
- `root` the rootNode of the json-editor and
- `editor` the current editor instance

_Only supported by useEditor_
properties.name: onChange

type.text: (data: Data, root: JsonNode, editor: HeadlessEditor<Data>) => void

parameter.text: ArrayNode | ObjectNode | FileNode | StringNode | NumberNode | BooleanNode | NullNode
parameter.name: root

methods.comment: destroys editor instance including all plugins, releasing used memory.

properties.comment:
The list of available widgets to render the JSON Schema tree. Each node in the tree should
have a matching widget to be rendered or the editor will either ignore or show an error
using an Error Widget if added.

A unique id that if changed, will recreate the editor

_Only supported by useEditor_
